findings.title: Thin-market donation inflation lets an attacker steal later deposits

best_agent_match.title: Thin-market donation inflation lets an attacker steal later deposits

findings.title: Borrow and redeem transfer out underlying before updating debt or collateral, enabling cross-market reentrancy with callback tokens

best_agent_match.title: Borrow and redeem transfer out underlying before updating debt/collateral, enabling cross-market reentrancy with callback tokens

findings.title: Small `redeemUnderlying` calls can withdraw underlying while burning zero cTokens

best_agent_match.title: Small `redeemUnderlying` calls can withdraw underlying while burning zero cTokens

rejected_candidates.title: Flash Loan Attack on Exchange Rate During Mint
rejected_candidates.reason: Misreads the code path: `mintFresh()` reads the exchange rate before `doTransferIn()` at line 1594, not after. The real issue is donation-driven exchange-rate inflation, which is captured separately.

rejected_candidates.title: Flash Loan Attack on Exchange Rate During Redeem
rejected_candidates.reason: Changing cash after `redeemFresh()` computes `redeemAmount` does not increase the already-fixed payout. The plausible issue here is stale-state reentrancy during `doTransferOut()`, captured separately.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Zero-Address Validation on Initialize
rejected_candidates.reason: Initializing with zero comptroller or interest model does not silently succeed: `_setComptroller()` and `_setInterestRateModelFresh()` call marker methods that revert on invalid addresses. This is not a distinct exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: Unsafe ERC-20 Approval Pattern
rejected_candidates.reason: This is the standard ERC-20 allowance overwrite behavior on the cToken share token, explicitly documented in the code comments. It is a known user-level caveat, not a protocol-specific vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Admin Can Set Arbitrary Initial Exchange Rate
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a trusted deployment/configuration parameter chosen at market creation, not a permissionless exploit against deployed users.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential State Manipulation Through Block Delta
rejected_candidates.reason: Depends on mocked or test-only `getBlockNumber()` behavior rather than a realistic deployed attack path.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Event Emission on Initialize
rejected_candidates.reason: Operational/informational observation only; no realistic protocol harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Deprecated Solidity Version
rejected_candidates.reason: Generic technical-debt note, not a concrete vulnerability in this codebase.
